visualGoal: Make the mounting ladder legible at a glance so a reader lands on the right rung (box clamps for one yoke, aluminum mount for a HOTAS, powered hub for panels, frame for FFB) without over-buying. This is a low-product-density how-to: real product proof only where genuinely relevant; carry the rest of the visual load with infographics/diagrams, not forced product cards.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Powered 10-port USB hub (sim-cockpit style, dedicated adapter + per-port switches)
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Level-3 hero product. Previously embedded here with an UNVERIFIED image; now text+Amazon only. No /flight/gear/ page exists for it (getStaticPaths gates on verified image), so a gearpick 'Spec card' link would 404 until a photo lands. Source a real photo before it can become a body visual.

The FFB yoke that triggers Level 4 (a real frame). Previously embedded with an UNVERIFIED image; now text+Amazon only. No /flight/gear/ page exists until a verified photo is added. Also referenced in force-feedback-yoke-worth-it — a shared verified MOZA photo would unblock multiple articles.

realProductPhotosNeeded.product: Under-desk mounting plate (Honeycomb-style, e.g. FlightSimBuilder / Flight Velocity)
realProductPhotosNeeded.notes: Not currently in the body. A real under-desk plate photo would let us add a genuine Level-1.5 visual (space-saving clamp-free option) without inventing product cards. Low priority.

editorialCompositePrompts.prompt: Realistic flight-sim desk at cool cyan rim light against a navy background, a Honeycomb-style yoke clamped to a solid desk edge in the foreground with the steel desk clamp clearly visible underneath, a rigid anodized-aluminum arm holding a throttle beside it, and a hint of an 8020 aluminum-profile frame receding into the background to imply the escalation, cable tray visible, no fake logos, no fake UI, 16:9 editorial cover. Space left clear at the desk edge for a real yoke product overlay.
editorialCompositePrompts.negativePrompt: fake logos, floating hardware, warped clamps, impossible ergonomics, tiny unreadable text, procedural gradient art

infographicPrompts.title: The flight sim mounting ladder
content: Rung 1 — Box clamps (one yoke + throttle): use what shipped; clamp a solid edge; pad delicate tops
Rung 2 — Rigid aluminum desk mount (HOTAS / 2-3 pieces): ~$100 each, two per HOTAS
Rung 3 — Powered USB hub (3-4+ devices): ~$60, own adapter, power management OFF
Rung 4 — Cockpit frame / 8020 rig (pedals + panels + FFB): ~$499 prebuilt / ~$325+ DIY parts
infographicPrompts.style: IgnitionSim command-center numbered staircase, large readable text, flight cyan/navy palette, one small real product photo per rung as a row marker for Codex overlay (Alpha, Monstertech mount, USB-hub placeholder, Trak Racer TR80)

infographicPrompts.title: Do you need a powered USB hub?
content: 1-2 devices -> direct to PC USB 2.0 ports
5+ devices / any multi-panel stack -> powered hub MANDATORY (own wall adapter)
Panel drops out mid-flight? -> brownout or Windows power management, not a dead panel
Fixes: powered hub, USB selective-suspend OFF, older Saitek gear on USB 2.0
infographicPrompts.style: Decision-tree flow, flight palette, callout box for the 'panel keeps disappearing' failure mode; no product photos required

curatorScenePrompts.prompt: Realistic adult flight-sim curator Mac Donovan crouched at a desk edge, one hand snugging a steel yoke clamp against a solid desktop, thin protective pad visible between clamp and surface, calm hands-on-hardware energy, practical home setup not a fantasy cockpit, product overlay space beside him, 4:5 portrait.
